uebersicht.text: Back to Balance is an economic system built on the fundamental principles of equal dignity, sustainability and non-violence. Not as a utopia – but as a concrete model that builds on two proven legal forms, calculates with real numbers and is honest about what it cannot yet deliver in its founding phase.
uebersicht.wirtschaft_text: In the conventional market, fair wages and affordable prices are structurally contradictory. In the BtB system, they are the natural consequence: wages come from the pool, product prices cover only material costs.
uebersicht.gemeinschaft_text: Self-governed cells, a solidarity fund for pregnancy and illness, rhythm recognition and a consequence system instead of punishment – structure that sustains.
uebersicht.international_text: New cells worldwide join the universe – under the same charter, with access to the shared app. Not a copy, but part of the system.

wirtschaft.untertitel: Fair wages and affordable prices – simultaneously
wirtschaft.intro: In the conventional market, wages are a cost item passed on in the product price. Every wage increase makes the product more expensive. Fair wages and affordable prices are structurally contradictory – this is not a failure of the market, it is its logic. Within the Back to Balance system, this does not apply internally.
wirtschaft.pool_text: All income flows into a shared pool. What used to be borne alone as fixed costs is now carried collectively. If you earn 1,000 euros alone, you pay all costs yourself. If you earn 1,000 euros in the pool, you share those same costs with others – and get purchase prices on everything available in the system.
wirtschaft.pool_detail: The internal product price covers only external material costs – it contains no labour. Labour is compensated through the pool, goods cost only their actual material purchase price. The higher the hourly rate rises, the more everyone earns – and internal products still don't get more expensive. Real purchasing power rises disproportionately with every new offering in the system.
wirtschaft.dreiteilung_text: The generated surplus is divided into three equal parts:
wirtschaft.dreiteilung_verguetung: Compensation – flows as coin reserves to the members. No profession is worth more than another.
wirtschaft.dreiteilung_reinvest: Reinvestment – fixed costs, infrastructure, growth. Tax advisor, insurance, software – everything is carried by the system.
wirtschaft.dreiteilung_sozial: Social & ecological share – solidarity fund, community projects, ecological initiatives. A system that only feeds itself has no future.
wirtschaft.stundensatz_text: One hour is one hour. Whether you cook, build, heal, teach, care, or program – everyone receives the same hourly rate. This is not just an economic model – it is a statement about what work means and who deserves dignity. The target hourly rate is 20 euros net – an average of lower and middle class wages in Germany, enabling a good life without luxury.
wirtschaft.coins_text: One BtB Coin always corresponds to exactly one hour worked. This principle is immutable. Each coin entry carries the euro settlement value of the period in which it was earned – this prevents speculation through hoarding.
wirtschaft.coins_detail: Internally, you buy everything at purchase price with coins – food, services, housing. If you need euros, you request a payout at the historical settlement value. Every outstanding coin is a liability of the cooperative – the euro equivalent is available for payout at all times.
wirtschaft.coins_itadakimasu: In Japanese, there is a word spoken before every meal: Itadakimasu – literally: I humbly receive. A thanks to all who have ever contributed. Doing business with the coin as time accounting is lived Itadakimasu.
wirtschaft.modell_titel: Model Calculations – Does the Math Work?
wirtschaft.modell_text: When the system is fully developed, 47 hours per month at 20 €/h cover the entire cost of living – food for 90 instead of 380 euros, housing for 380 instead of 850 euros. During the build-up phase, while rent and utilities still come from the external market, more hours are needed – just like in any regular employment. At 20 €/h net and full-time, that is 3,460 € per month – enough for market rent, living costs and reserves. Once the cooperative owns its own properties, external cost pressure drops and the required hours approach the target of 47.
wirtschaft.modell_phase1: Seed phase (Month 1–3): 0 €/h – citizen's benefit as a bridge, laying the foundation
wirtschaft.modell_phase2: First income (Month 3–9): ~8 €/h – pooling resources, shortfall halved
wirtschaft.modell_phase3: Turning point (Month 6–12): €1,170 external revenue is enough for break-even
wirtschaft.modell_phase5: Target value (Year 4–6): 20 €/h – when ¾ external and good profession mix
wirtschaft.ek_text: The purchasing power gain through purchase prices is not theory. It arises because wages and product prices are decoupled: labour is compensated through the pool, goods cost only their actual material purchase price. Those who live in the system never buy at market price – and this applies to all internal services and products: food, craftsmanship, bodywork, education, repairs, housing, technology. Real purchasing power rises disproportionately with every new offering – because every additional internally available service reduces dependence on the external market and amplifies the effect of all other internal offerings. For people on citizen's benefit, this is transformative: 100 euros saved on 563 euros income means 18% more purchasing power – without earning a single euro more.
wirtschaft.geringverdiener_titel: Why It Has the Strongest Impact for Low Earners
wirtschaft.geringverdiener_text: Someone with 563 euros gains 100 euros through purchase prices – that is transformative. 18% more purchasing power without earning a single euro more. The system is not a luxury for people who could afford to be alone. It is a structure built for people who cannot afford it.
wirtschaft.mittelschicht_titel: Why the Middle Class Also Benefits
wirtschaft.mittelschicht_text: A skilled worker earning 22 euros gross per hour takes home about 15 euros net. In the BtB system, they receive 20 euros net – and buy everything internally at purchase prices: food, craftsmanship, housing, services. Real purchasing power rises far beyond what is possible in the conventional system. The effect: fewer working hours for the same standard of living – or the same effort for significantly more quality of life. The more middle class people join the system, the broader the range of offerings, the stronger the collective economic power – and the more obsolete a system built on competition and profit maximisation becomes.
wirtschaft.megafirma_text: What looks like a small community from the outside is, on the inside, a legal entity with the full market power of a large company. One business liability insurance for all. One organic certificate for the cooperative as a whole. Democratic structure as protection against external takeover.
wirtschaft.megafirma_unesco: The cooperative is the oldest and most proven form of collective self-help in Germany – declared an intangible cultural heritage by UNESCO. Back to Balance uses this form not out of tradition, but because it works.

gemeinschaft.intro: A system that outwardly practices sustainability and equal dignity, but is internally driven by ego, fear and power games, is not an alternative. It is a repetition. The inner practice is not decoration – it is the foundation.
gemeinschaft.zellen_text: The cooperative organises itself in self-governed cells. Each cell operates independently and settles services according to shared principles. The profits earned by each cell flow into the shared umbrella pool – where the trisection applies. The general assembly does not interfere in cell-internal matters. The cell knows its people.
gemeinschaft.zellen_detail: Reinvestment and social shares are distributed equally across all cells – regardless of their size or revenue. Every cell is fertilised equally. A strong cell thus automatically supports a weaker one.
gemeinschaft.solidar_text: The solidarity fund covers pregnancy, parental leave, rhythm days, chronic illness and other situations recognised by the system. It is financed from the social and ecological share of the pool – 10% flows into the fund monthly.
gemeinschaft.solidar_rhythmus: Rhythm recognition: The universe acknowledges that not all members have the same capacity every day. For people with a menstrual cycle, up to 3 rhythm days per month are available. Pregnancy is accompanied with additional hours from the 4th month. Parental leave is secured on a graduated basis over 24 months.
gemeinschaft.solidar_kapazitaet: Capacity justice: For chronic illness, up to five capacity days per month are available – without proof required. For permanently reduced capacity, there is an individual agreement: no minimum hour contribution, full hourly rate for every hour worked.
gemeinschaft.gleichwuerdigkeit_text: Everyone who is part of the system has a voice – not because they have contributed equally, but because they are human. The deposit is not refunded upon departure. It remains as a contribution to the common good.
gemeinschaft.gleichwuerdigkeit_transparenz: Hour transparency within the cell: All recorded hours are visible to other cell members. This is not a control system – it is mutual visibility as a culture of trust. The principle: control from below, not from above.
gemeinschaft.konsequenz_text: The system knows no punishment in the sense of retaliation. It knows consequences – feedback loops that restore balance. A member's basic provision – housing, food, basic medical care – is untouchable by any consequence. This is non-negotiable.
gemeinschaft.konsequenz_stufen: 7 levels: Clarification talk → Guided pause → Voting rights suspension → Share reduction (reversible) → Reduction of additional benefits → Restorative work → Exclusion (70% majority). A cell that fails is not excluded – it is supported.
gemeinschaft.stufen_text: The system deliberately distinguishes between three levels of belonging – not as a hierarchy, but as an honest description of what someone currently contributes.
gemeinschaft.stufen_unterstuetzer: Supporter: Free time, skills, at least €50 donation per year. In return: 10% discount on all external services.
gemeinschaft.stufen_anwaerter: Candidate: More time, participation in concrete projects. In return: purchase prices on selected services, guidance.
gemeinschaft.stufen_vollmitglied: Full member: Daily life, working hours, skills, time tracking. In return: full compensation, solidarity fund, voting rights, co-creation.
gemeinschaft.bildung_text: In the conventional system, grades, Numerus Clausus and state licences determine who may practise a profession. Inside the Back to Balance Universe a different principle applies: if you can do it, you may do it. Within the cooperative, members provide services to one another – as cooperative mutual aid. No external licensing restrictions apply to internal activities.
gemeinschaft.bildung_nachweis: Open access instead of Numerus Clausus. Internal proof of competence through practice and mentoring instead of state examinations. Experienced members accompany and confirm ability instead of official accreditation. Services are settled internally via Coins. Learning is work – compensated like any other hour.
gemeinschaft.bildung_kinder: Children can be children again. No performance pressure from year 3 for the grammar-school transition. No grade-point optimisation from year 10. Learning from curiosity, not from fear of the grade average. Anyone who wants to study medicine at 30 can do so – without competing against 18-year-olds with a 1.0 average.
gemeinschaft.bildung_kollektiv: A system that only lets the supposedly best through excludes 90 % of competence. Ten specialists give a patient ten different diagnoses. Twenty practitioners who exchange ideas recognise patterns. More eyes see more. The Back to Balance Universe relies on collective competence rather than individual elite performance.
gemeinschaft.bildung_grenze: Externally, all legal requirements continue to apply. Anyone practising outside the system needs the corresponding licence. Internally the rule is: competence before certificate, mentoring before examination, access before selection.
gemeinschaft.nicht_kommunismus: Not communism: In communism, property is forcibly collectivised – from above, through power. The Back to Balance Universe is the opposite. Every step is voluntary. Nobody is forced to share. And whoever leaves, leaves freely.
gemeinschaft.nicht_anarchismus: Not anarchism: The system has rules, a charter, organs, voting processes, consequences. It is a democratically constituted economic community.
gemeinschaft.nicht_investment: Not an investment vehicle: Cooperative shares cannot be traded. Whoever invests does so as a commitment – not as an investment.
gemeinschaft.wohnraum_text: Once the cooperative owns or long-term leases its own properties, rooms and apartments are allocated to members through a transparent priority system. Rent covers only the running costs of the property – no profit margin.
gemeinschaft.wohnraum_stufe1: Priority 1 – Acute need: Homeless members, eviction notice received, domestic violence. Securing existence comes first.
gemeinschaft.wohnraum_stufe2: Priority 2 – Families: Members with children or expecting parents. Stability for the next generation.
gemeinschaft.wohnraum_stufe3: Priority 3 – Special needs: Chronic illness, disability, caring for dependents. Capacity justice also applies to housing.
gemeinschaft.wohnraum_stufe4: Priority 4 – Seniority: Full members by duration of membership. Those who helped build the system have priority.
gemeinschaft.wohnraum_stufe5: Priority 5 – Waiting list: All other full members in order of registration.
gemeinschaft.wohnraum_detail: Within the same priority level, duration of membership decides. Allocation is decided by the respective cell. Those who receive internal housing free up external housing – reducing the overall need for market rent within the system. The exact procedure is determined by the general assembly and can be adapted to local conditions.

international.intro: The Back to Balance Universe does not want to displace anything. The euro remains the external conversion currency. What is aspired to is a worldwide network under one shared system.
international.vision_text: International cells in the early phase run their own local pool in the home currency – fully part of the network: they vote, share knowledge, build modules. Full accession happens when three conditions are met: the German core cell is self-sustaining, a legal framework for cross-border transfers is clarified, and the international cell has demonstrated at least three periods of stable pool income.
international.einheit_titel: Unified System Instead of Open Source
international.einheit_text: An obvious idea would be: make the charter, app code and concept freely available for other cooperatives. This idea was examined and deliberately rejected. Five parallel 'BtB-like' cooperatives are weaker than one with five cells. The impact comes from unity – not from proliferation.
international.einheit_detail: New cells worldwide join the Back to Balance Universe – under the same charter, with access to the shared app and the shared knowledge pool. They are part of the system, not a copy of it. That is the difference between strength and dispersal.
international.zellen_weltweit_text: Every new cell is a node in a worldwide network. Self-governed, but connected. The regulatory responsibility lies with each cell – it knows its local laws, its local needs, its local people.
international.politische_wirkung_text: The Back to Balance Universe has no political mission in the party-political sense. It does not position itself, it does not demand, it does not fight against. What it does is stronger: it exists. When 1,000 people live in this model and show that it works – that changes the societal discourse. Not through persuasion. Through demonstration.
international.sinnmaximierung_text: This term comes from Samuel Waldeck, founder of Shiftphone. 'The complete opposite for us is: meaning maximisation. And if you had to sum it up in one word, it would be: appreciation. On all levels, as best we can.'
